Title: Rewiring of the seed metabolome during Tartary buckwheat domestication

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Plant defence signaling molecule; systemic acquired resistance
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Salicylic acid glycoside hydrolysis / salicylic acid homeostasis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydrolyzes salicylic acid 2-O-β-glucoside to release free salicylic acid
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control point for active SA release; not formally established as rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes SA accumulation and decreases SAG; enhanced disease resistance when overexpressed
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. mGWAS and domestication sweep overlap identified FtSAGH1 as the candidate gene associated with SA variation during domestication. 2. FtSAGH1 expression positively correlated with SA content, and the domestication-associated low-SA allele increased in cultivated SL accessions. 3. Recombinant FtSAGH1 hydrolyzed SAG to SA in vitro, and overexpression increased SA and reduced SAG in hairy roots. 4. Ectopic overexpression enhanced resistance to Rhizoctonia solani, linking SA metabolism to defence-related domestication effects.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Prominent in stem, leaf and mature seed
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Prominent in stem, leaf and mature seed in buckwheat
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: mGWAS; domestication sweep colocalization; expression-metabolite correlation; recombinant protein enzyme assay in E. coli; hairy-root overexpression with metabolite profiling; ectopic overexpression in Arabidopsis with disease assay
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Widely targeted metabolomics by LC-MS/MS; metabolite GWAS using resequencing-derived SNPs (1,004,824 SNPs)
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Domestication group comparison (HW vs SL); Rhizoctonia solani AG-4 HGI infection
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target to raise active SA and improve disease resistance in Tartary buckwheat; should be considered for marker-assisted selection or overexpression/editing to restore defence-related metabolite levels lost during domestication
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Higher SA and related defence metabolites were discussed as potentially negatively correlated with grain yield and growth-related domestication traits
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Two nearby glycoside hydrolase genes, FtPinG0707406600.01 and FtPinG0707407400.01, did not show detectable SAG hydrolysis under the tested conditions

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Improved solubility and bioavailability of emodin; medicinally relevant compound in Polygonaceae
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers glucose to emodin to form EmG
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key terminal determinant for EmG accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes EmG accumulation; reduced expression during domestication is associated with lower EmG in cultivated accessions
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. mGWAS and domestication sweep overlap identified FtUGT74L2 as the major candidate gene for EmG variation. 2. FtUGT74L2 expression positively correlated with EmG content across haplotypes. 3. Recombinant FtUGT74L2 converted emodin to EmG in vitro. 4. Hairy-root overexpression increased EmG, supporting its in vivo role in emodin glycosylation and domestication-associated reduction of glycosylated emodin.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: mGWAS; domestication sweep colocalization; expression-metabolite correlation; recombinant protein enzyme assay in E. coli; hairy-root overexpression with LC-MS metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Widely targeted metabolomics by LC-MS/MS; metabolite GWAS using resequencing-derived SNPs (1,004,824 SNPs)
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate for metabolic engineering or marker-assisted selection to increase glycosylated emodin content and medicinal quality in Tartary buckwheat
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: EmG and related specialized metabolites were reported to be negatively correlated with grain yield in this population
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The lower-EmG allele increased in frequency from wild Himalayan to cultivated SL accessions, indicating negative selection of glycosylated emodin during domestication

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Buckwheat soluble carbohydrate associated with desiccation tolerance; potential relevance to non-insulin dependent diabetes mellitus
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Phenylpropanoid entry enzyme linked by association to fagopyritol A1 variation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Associated with FPA1 content variation during domestication; causal mechanism remains unclear
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. An mGWAS signal for fagopyritol A1 overlapped a domestication sweep, and FtPAL was the nearby candidate gene. 2. FtPAL expression varied between haplotypes associated with FPA1 content. 3. The paper suggests FtPAL may be involved in FPA1 change during domestication, but the biosynthetic mechanism is unresolved.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Varies between haplotypes; detailed tissue pattern NA
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: mGWAS; domestication sweep colocalization; haplotype-expression association
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Widely targeted metabolomics by LC-MS/MS; metabolite GWAS using resequencing-derived SNPs (1,004,824 SNPs)
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Tentative marker candidate for manipulating fagopyritol-related nutritional traits, but requires functional validation before deployment
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Authors explicitly note that fagopyritol biosynthesis is unclear and that the mechanism of PAL regulating FPA1 needs further study

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Bioactive phenolic compounds with nutritional and medicinal value
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: FtDFR; FtANS; FtFLS; AtANS; AtDFR; AtTT10
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Positive regulator of procyanidin biosynthesis identified by mGWAS and functional validation
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. mGWAS mapped procyanidin B1 variation to a locus upstream of FtMYB43, and gene expression positively correlated with procyanidin content. 2. FtMYB43 encodes an R2R3-MYB with transcriptional activation activity. 3. Overexpression in buckwheat hairy roots upregulated FtDFR, FtANS and FtFLS. 4. Ectopic expression in Arabidopsis increased expression of AtANS, AtDFR and AtTT10 and increased total procyanidins.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expression level positively correlates with procyanidin B1 content across accessions
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expression level positively correlates with procyanidin B1 content across accessions
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: mGWAS; expression-metabolite correlation; phylogenetic analysis; yeast auto-activation assay; hairy-root overexpression with qRT-PCR; ectopic expression in Arabidopsis with metabolite assay
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Widely targeted metabolomics by LC-MS/MS; metabolite GWAS using resequencing-derived SNPs (1,004,824 SNPs)
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising regulator for engineering or selecting higher procyanidin content and improved nutraceutical quality in Tartary buckwheat
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Procyanidin A3 and other specialized metabolites showed negative correlation with grain yield in the population analysis
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Homologues were previously implicated in anthocyanin or rutin regulation, supporting the inferred flavonoid-regulatory role

Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Candidate regulator associated with domestication-related decrease of C7G, but not functionally proven
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Putatively associated with reduced C7G during domestication
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. A C7G-associated mGWAS signal overlapped a domestication sweep in NL. 2. Nearby FtPinG0303261500.01 encodes a NAC transcription factor whose expression differed between haplotypes. 3. The paper proposes that this NAC factor might be involved in the domestication-related decrease of C7G, but direct functional validation was not provided.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: mGWAS; domestication sweep colocalization; haplotype-expression association
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Widely targeted metabolomics by LC-MS/MS; metabolite GWAS using resequencing-derived SNPs (1,004,824 SNPs)
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential marker candidate for C7G-associated flavonoid quality traits, but requires direct functional validation before use
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: This gene is a candidate rather than a fully validated regulator in this paper
